description_long: This is an American football with a classic prolate spheroid shape, featuring a textured brown surface that mimics the look of genuine leather. The surface is covered in a prominent pebble-grain pattern, providing enhanced grip for throwing and catching. Two wide white stripes encircle the ball near each end, a common design for increased visibility during play. The football lacks visible laces or branding, giving it a clean and generic appearance. The object appears to be inflated, suggesting an internal air bladder beneath the outer shell. This type of football is typically used in parks, playgrounds, or urban recreational areas.
description: A brown, pebble-textured American football with two white stripes and no visible branding or laces. The surface is grippy and designed for easy handling.
description_view_0: End-on view showing the rounded tip and a single white stripe encircling the ball's circumference.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the football, both white stripes, and the detailed pebble-grain texture.
description_view_2: Opposite end-on view, similar to view 0, with the other white stripe visible.
description_view_3: Side view mirroring view 1, again showing both white stripes and the textured brown surface.
